objective: Sweep the last 24h for SSH lateral movement between internal hosts
narrative: Reviewed 41 SSH sessions across HOST_02, HOST_05, and HOST_11 in the window. Every session authenticated to a known service account, no host pair was seen for the first time, and there were no failed-then-success bursts that would signal credential spraying. No lateral movement was found.

findings.title: SSH activity stays within known service accounts
findings.detail: All 41 Zeek SSH sessions authenticated to service accounts already established on these hosts. No first-seen host-to-host pair appeared, so there is no evidence of an operator pivoting between machines over SSH.

findings.title: No failed-then-success authentication bursts
findings.detail: A search for failed SSH authentications immediately preceding a success (the signature of credential spraying or brute force) returned zero matches across the window.

report.narrative: Reviewed 41 SSH sessions across HOST_02, HOST_05, and HOST_11 in the window. Every session authenticated to a known service account, no host pair was seen for the first time, and there were no failed-then-success bursts that would signal credential spraying. No lateral movement was found.

recommended_actions.title: No action required
recommended_actions.rationale: SSH activity in this window is consistent with routine service-account operations; continue baseline monitoring.

objective: Hunt for DNS beaconing and algorithmically-generated domains in the last 24h
narrative: Aggregated outbound DNS by client. HOST_07 showed periodic queries to updates.example.com at a fixed 300-second cadence, which matched a known software-update poller rather than command-and-control. No high-entropy or algorithmically-generated domain names were observed anywhere in the window.

findings.title: Periodic DNS from HOST_07 is a known update poller
findings.detail: HOST_07 queried updates.example.com every 300 seconds. The fixed cadence and the well-known vendor update endpoint match a software-update client, not beaconing to C2. The destination resolves to a public CDN and carries no blocklist hits.

findings.title: No algorithmically-generated domains observed
findings.detail: Screening outbound DNS for high-entropy labels (the signature of DGA malware) returned no candidates across the window.

report.narrative: Aggregated outbound DNS by client. HOST_07 showed periodic queries to updates.example.com at a fixed 300-second cadence, which matched a known software-update poller rather than command-and-control. No high-entropy or algorithmically-generated domain names were observed anywhere in the window.

recommended_actions.title: Baseline the update poller
recommended_actions.rationale: Add updates.example.com to the known-good periodic-DNS baseline so future runs suppress it automatically.
